title: Education ERP Market to Reach USD 75.84 Billion by 2032 Fueled by Technological Innovations and Rising Institutional Demands | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The Education ERP market is projected to grow significantly, driven by technological advancements and increasing demand for streamlined administrative processes in educational institutions. The market is expected to reach USD 75.84 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 18.46% from 2024 to 2032.

title: KKR, Francisco Partners vying to acquire Instructure, sources say By Reuters - Investing.com
author: Oliver Gray
description: Private equity firms KKR and Francisco Partners are reportedly in the final round of bidding to acquire education software provider Instructure. However, the current majority stakeholder, Thoma Bravo, may not agree to sell its 83% stake, and other potential bidders could still enter the race.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: KKR is actively bidding to acquire Instructure, indicating interest in the company.
KKR is actively bidding to acquire Instructure, indicating interest in the company.
Instructure is the target of the acquisition, but the outcome is uncertain as the current majority stakeholder may not agree to sell.

author: Milana Vinn

title: Instructure Holdings Stock Is Rising: What's Going On?
author: Adam Eckert
description:
Shares of education technology firm Instructure Holdings Inc (NYSE:INST) are trading higher Tuesday on above-average volume despite a lack of company-specific news. Here’s a look at what’s going on.
What To Know: Instructure Holdings shares were up more than 6% at one point Tuesday afternoon, adding to gains of nearly 24% over the last month. The stock began moving higher in early May after the company reported first-quarter financial results and issued upbeat guidance for the second quarter.
